title: Cellphone ban draws Provincial Minister of Education to Point High
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"MOSSEL BAY NEWS - The Western Cape Minister of Education, David Maynier, paid a visit to Point High School on Thursday 30 October, as a result of the school's recent decision to extend its policy that bans cellphones.","html":"MOSSEL BAY NEWS - The Western Cape Minister of Education, David Maynier, paid a visit to Point High School on Thursday 30 October, as a result of the school's recent decision to extend its policy that bans cellphones."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The minister was interested to hear more about the school's reasons for this decision, as well as how it went about implementing the policy."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"It is encouraging to hear that the school has seen im-provements in learner results and social interactions as a result of the new approach, and there are lessons that other schools can draw on if they are considering implementing a similar cellphone-free zone,\" he said.","html":"\"It is encouraging to hear that the school has seen im-provements in learner results and social interactions as a result of the new approach, and there are lessons that other schools can draw on if they are considering implementing a similar cellphone-free zone,\" he said."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Maynier was accompanied by the Western Cape's Education Circuit manager, Rozelle Smith-Neethling, as well as Jewel Jonkers, director of the Eden and Central Karoo Education Department (Ecked).","html":"Maynier was accompanied by the Western Cape's Education Circuit manager, Rozelle Smith-Neethling, as well as Jewel Jonkers, director of the Eden and Central Karoo Education Department (Ecked)."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"In a previous Facebook post sharing the news, the school wrote that the policy, which became effective on 3 September, aims to strengthen academic focus, promote social interaction and protect the learners' mental health."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"Point High has demonstrated that it is possible to practically and responsibly reduce this impact while taking into account safety concerns and e-learning needs,\" said Maynier.","html":"\"Point High has demonstrated that it is possible to practically and responsibly reduce this impact while taking into account safety concerns and e-learning needs,\" said Maynier."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The news has sparked debate among the Mossel Bay community, with many stating why they are for or against the school's decision.","html":"The news has sparked debate among the Mossel Bay community, with many stating why they are for or against the school's decision."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Cellphone ban draws Provincial Minister of Education to Point High
